title: phyllanthus reticulatus
intro: Phyllanthus reticulatus, also known as the manchineel tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and is known for its toxic and medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Phyllanthus reticulatus can be found in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
